message:
fix: eigenvector_centrality{,_numpy} raise on empty graph (br-r37-c1-zvfcu)

fnx.eigenvector_centrality(empty_graph) and
fnx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(empty_graph) silently returned ``{}``.
nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept('cannot compute centrality for the
null graph'). Drop-in code that does
``pytest.raises(NetworkXPointlessConcept, ...)`` failed under fnx.

Repro:
    >>> fnx.eigenvector_centrality(fnx.Graph())
    {}
    >>> nx.eigenvector_centrality(nx.Graph())
    NetworkXPointlessConcept: cannot compute centrality for the null graph

Survey of other centralities on empty graphs (degree, in_degree,
out_degree, closeness, betweenness, harmonic, load, subgraph,
communicability_betweenness, information, current_flow_*, pagerank,
hits, katz, katz_numpy, laplacian) all already matched nx's
empty-graph behavior — only the two eigenvector variants were the
remaining outliers.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:
- ``eigenvector_centrality``: add explicit ``len(G) == 0`` guard
  raising NetworkXPointlessConcept with nx's exact wording, before
  the kwarg dispatch / Rust fast-path branches.
- ``eigenvector_centrality_numpy``: replace the
  ``if n == 0: return {}`` early-return with the same raise.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_eigenvector_empty_graph_parity.py``:
- test_eigenvector_centrality_empty_raises_pointlessconcept
  (exact-match wording on both fnx and nx)
- test_eigenvector_centrality_numpy_empty_raises_pointlessconcept
- test_eigenvector_centrality_empty_caught_by_nx_class
  (verifies fnx exception is catchable via nx.NetworkXPointlessConcept)
- test_eigenvector_centrality_non_empty_still_works
  (regression guard for the happy path)
- test_eigenvector_centrality_numpy_non_empty_still_works

Full sweep
----------
6648 passed, 0 failed (was 6643 before — +5 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-r37-c1-z2hm3","title":"parity: geometric_soft_configuration_graph silently ran wrong algorithm (random_geometric_graph instead of S^1/H^2 model)","description":"fnx had geometric_soft_configuration_graph(beta=1, n=100, dim=2, pos=None, seed=None) which silently re-routed to random_geometric_graph(n, 0.3) — a completely different model. nx's S^1/H^2 hyperbolic soft configuration model uses signature (*, beta, n=None, gamma=None, mean_degree=None, kappas=None, seed=None) where beta is required. Drop-in callers expecting the documented Krioukov S^1 model got back a uniform random geometric graph (no degree heterogeneity, no hyperbolic structure, wrong density/clustering). Aligned the signature to nx exactly and delegated the implementation to nx so the well-tested upstream algorithm runs end-to-end. 8 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:21:19.401830126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:22:06.791575501Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:22:06.789049518Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 83b29d5. Drop-in code that calls eulerian_path(digraph) breaks. Same pattern as br-r37-c1-bf1wb (has_eulerian_path). Fix: delegate to nx for directed inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:45:43.895735626Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:47:52.257570848Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:47:52.255437959Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 05cc661. eulerian_path now accepts directed graphs (delegating to nx for the directed case), matching nx's full contract. 9 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-zud42","title":"parity: community louvain_partitions missing (exposes per-level Louvain hierarchy)","description":"networkx.algorithms.community.louvain_partitions(G, weight=weight, resolution=1, threshold=1e-7, seed=None) yields partitions at each Louvain level. franken_networkx exposes louvain_communities (final partition only) but is missing louvain_partitions which gives intermediate levels for inspection/debugging. Add Python wrapper delegating via _call_networkx_community_for_parity, export at top-level and add to __all__.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T13:47:44.957693619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T14:22:43.059355524Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T14:22:43.057527854Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9b2ff10: louvain_partitions wrapper; verified karate seed=42 -> 2 levels, partition contents identical to nx","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Drop-in code that does pytest.raises(NetworkXPointlessConcept) won't trigger on fnx.

Repro:
  >>> fnx.eigenvector_centrality(fnx.Graph())
  {}
  >>> nx.eigenvector_centrality(nx.Graph())
  NetworkXPointlessConcept: cannot compute centrality for the null graph

Survey of other centralities on empty graphs (degree, in_degree, out_degree, closeness, betweenness, harmonic, load, subgraph, communicability_betweenness, information, current_flow_*, pagerank, hits, katz, katz_numpy) all match nx's behavior — only eigenvector_centrality and eigenvector_centrality_numpy are off.

Fix: add explicit empty-graph guard at the top of both wrappers that raises NetworkXPointlessConcept with nx's wording, before falling through to the Rust/numpy implementation that swallows the case.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:01.797714267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T05:44:33.169331648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-01","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] signature mismatches blocked nx drop-in kwarg calls (8 functions)","description":"A signature sweep comparing every fnx public function against its networkx counterpart surfaced real drop-in breakage. Missing nx-style kwargs caused TypeErrors on documented calls:

- fiedler_vector: missing tol, method, seed
- spectral_ordering: missing weight, tol, method, seed
- spectral_bisection: missing normalized, tol, method, seed
- incidence_matrix: missing keyword-only dtype
- attr_matrix: missing order (numpy memory layout) AND wrong return shape — fnx always returned a (matrix, labels) tuple, but nx returns just the matrix when rc_order is supplied
- attr_sparse_matrix: same return-shape defect as attr_matrix
- has_path: first positional named 'graph'; nx names it 'G' (broke has_path(G=x,...))
- harmonic_diameter / hyper_wiener_index: missing weight
- duplication_divergence_graph: missing create_using
- bfs_labeled_edges: parameter named 'source' (single) instead of nx's 'sources' (iterable)
- dfs_labeled_edges: missing keyword-only sort_neighbors
- is_connected_dominating_set: second param named 'S' instead of nx's 'nbunch'

All fixed to accept the same kwargs as networkx; where semantically meaningful (weight on wiener/harmonic, dtype on incidence_matrix, order on attr_matrix) the kwarg now affects the result. Others (tol/method/seed on the dense spectral solvers) are accepted for signature parity and noted as ignored by the deterministic numpy.linalg.eigh path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed signatures + return shapes. Verified each fixed function with nx-style kwargs; existing parity tests still pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-02","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] to_prufer_sequence panicked (Rust) on non-tree / bad labels instead of raising NotATree","description":"fnx.to_prufer_sequence(G) forwarded straight to the Rust helper which panicked with 'tree invariant: leaf must exist' when G wasn't a tree. networkx raises NotATree (a NetworkXException subclass) in that case, plus NetworkXPointlessConcept for <2 nodes and KeyError when node labels aren't {0..n-1}. PanicException bypasses ordinary except blocks in user code. Fixed by validating is_directed / node count / is_tree / node labels in Python before calling into Rust, matching nx's exception contract exactly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed: validate in Python, raise NetworkXPointlessConcept / NotATree / KeyError as appropriate; verified on karate_club_graph, empty graph, and string-labelled trees — all raise the same exception types as nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r38-c1-03","title":"REVIEW: [HIGH] bethe_hessian_matrix: wrong return type AND wrong values","description":"Two defects in fnx.bethe_hessian_matrix: (1) returned a legacy scipy.sparse.csr_matrix while nx returns csr_array — isinstance(r, csr_array) was False. (2) Computed A = adjacency(G, weight=None) and regularizer r from A.sum(axis=1), both unweighted, but nx uses the *weighted* adjacency for A and D and the *unweighted* degrees for r. On karate_club_graph (integer edge weights) fnx diverged from nx by up to ~465 in matrix entries. Fixed by building A via to_scipy_sparse_array(G, nodelist, format='csr') (weighted by default), computing r from G.degree(v) per-node (unweighted), and using sparse eye_array/diags_array throughout so the final product is a csr_array. Verified on karate, cycle5, K4, havel_hakimi; spectrum matches too.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T00:00:00Z","close_reason":"Fixed type (csr_array) and values. Verified across weighted and unweighted fixtures; spectrum matches; existing tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -4131,6 +4131,14 @@ def eigenvector_centrality(
     """
     _validate_backend_dispatch_keywords("eigenvector_centrality", backend, backend_kwargs)
 
+    # br-r37-c1-zvfcu: nx raises NetworkXPointlessConcept on the empty
+    # graph; the Rust fast path silently returned {}. Drop-in code that
+    # does ``pytest.raises(NetworkXPointlessConcept)`` failed under fnx.
+    if len(G) == 0:
+        raise NetworkXPointlessConcept(
+            "cannot compute centrality for the null graph"
+        )
+
     # br-eignstart: the Rust _raw_eigenvector_centrality raised
     # ``TypeError('franken_networkx currently only supports
     # nstart=None and weight=None...')`` when either kwarg was
@@ -25987,7 +25995,11 @@ def eigenvector_centrality_numpy(
     nodelist = list(G.nodes())
     n = len(nodelist)
     if n == 0:
-        return {}
+        # br-r37-c1-zvfcu: match nx's eigenvector_centrality_numpy
+        # which raises NetworkXPointlessConcept on the empty graph.
+        raise NetworkXPointlessConcept(
+            "cannot compute centrality for the null graph"
+        )
     A = to_numpy_array(G, nodelist=nodelist, weight=weight)
     vals, vecs = np.linalg.eig(A)
     idx = np.argmax(np.real(vals))

@@ -0,0 +1,92 @@
+"""Parity for eigenvector_centrality{,_numpy} on the empty graph.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-zvfcu. ``fnx.eigenvector_centrality(empty_graph)`` and
+``fnx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(empty_graph)`` silently returned
+``{}``; nx raises ``NetworkXPointlessConcept('cannot compute centrality
+for the null graph')``. Drop-in code that does
+``pytest.raises(NetworkXPointlessConcept, ...)`` failed under fnx.
+
+All other centralities (degree, in_degree, out_degree, closeness,
+betweenness, harmonic, load, subgraph, communicability_betweenness,
+information, current_flow_*, pagerank, hits, katz, katz_numpy) already
+matched nx's empty-graph behavior — only the two eigenvector variants
+were the remaining outliers.
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_eigenvector_centrality_empty_raises_pointlessconcept():
+    with pytest.raises(
+        fnx.NetworkXPointlessConcept,
+        match="cannot compute centrality for the null graph",
+    ):
+        fnx.eigenvector_centrality(fnx.Graph())
+    with pytest.raises(
+        nx.NetworkXPointlessConcept,
+        match="cannot compute centrality for the null graph",
+    ):
+        nx.eigenvector_centrality(nx.Graph())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_eigenvector_centrality_numpy_empty_raises_pointlessconcept():
+    with pytest.raises(
+        fnx.NetworkXPointlessConcept,
+        match="cannot compute centrality for the null graph",
+    ):
+        fnx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(fnx.Graph())
+    with pytest.raises(
+        nx.NetworkXPointlessConcept,
+        match="cannot compute centrality for the null graph",
+    ):
+        nx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(nx.Graph())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_eigenvector_centrality_empty_caught_by_nx_class():
+    """Drop-in: a fnx-raised NetworkXPointlessConcept must be catchable
+    via ``except nx.NetworkXPointlessConcept`` since fnx's exception
+    hierarchy is registered as a subclass of nx's."""
+    try:
+        fnx.eigenvector_centrality(fnx.Graph())
+    except nx.NetworkXPointlessConcept:
+        return
+    pytest.fail("fnx.eigenvector_centrality should raise on empty graph")
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_eigenvector_centrality_non_empty_still_works():
+    """Regression guard — the fix only adds an empty-graph guard;
+    the non-empty path must still produce correct values."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    Gn = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    f = fnx.eigenvector_centrality(G)
+    n = nx.eigenvector_centrality(Gn)
+    assert set(f.keys()) == set(n.keys())
+    for k in f:
+        assert abs(f[k] - n[k]) < 1e-6, (k, f[k], n[k])
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_eigenvector_centrality_numpy_non_empty_still_works():
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    Gn = nx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 1)])
+    f = fnx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(G)
+    n = nx.eigenvector_centrality_numpy(Gn)
+    assert set(f.keys()) == set(n.keys())
+    for k in f:
+        assert abs(f[k] - n[k]) < 1e-6, (k, f[k], n[k])
